content: Hi, I'm Jules. I'm a mechanical engineer at Tesla, and I'm really passionate about volunteer firefighting in my free time.
That's amazing! Volunteer firefighting is such a noble pursuit. What inspired you to get into it?

I've always been interested in helping others, and I think it's a great way to give back to my community. I'm also working on a side project - a community garden in my neighborhood in Oakland, California.
A community garden is a great idea! It's a wonderful way to bring people together and promote sustainability. How's the project going so far?
It's going well, thanks for asking! We've got a team of about 10 people working on it, and we're planning to launch it on April 1st. I'm really excited to see it come together.
That sounds like a great plan! Have you had any experience with gardening or community projects before?

Yeah, I've been involved in a few community projects in the past, and I've also done some travel blogging on the side. I love exploring new places and trying new foods - I'm a big fan of Japanese cuisine.
Travel blogging sounds like a lot of fun! What's been your favorite travel destination so far?
I think my favorite destination has been Tokyo, Japan. The food, the culture, the people - everything was just so amazing. I went there for 2 weeks in 2019 and had the time of my life.
Tokyo is a great city! I've heard the food is amazing. What kind of dishes did you try while you were there?
I tried all sorts of things - sushi, ramen, tempura... my favorite was probably the tonkatsu at this little restaurant in Shibuya. I'm planning to go back to Japan next year, maybe in June or July.
That sounds delicious! Tonkatsu is a great dish. Do you have any plans for your community garden project after it launches?

Yeah, we're planning to have a big launch event on April 1st, and then we'll be having regular gardening workshops and events throughout the year. I'm also thinking of starting a blog to document our progress and share tips with others.
That's a great idea! A blog can be a wonderful way to share your knowledge and connect with others who are interested in gardening and community projects.
